Russell E-mini - Individual Market Analysis

CoT signals and diagnostics for Russell E-mini futures.

As of 2026-04-07

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec_Net_%OI of -3.5%, a Spec_zscore of 0.63, and a 4-week flow of 3.4, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation (1).
  • The signal has transitioned into a Balanced_Long_Bias regime following a period of persistent short bias (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Improving; Threshold Proximity: Far; Revision Sensitivity: Moderate.

Methodology Applied:

  • Flow opposing Net Position direction indicates conviction fading among dominant participants, signaling potential trend reversal (1).
  • Speculator Z-Scores between -2 and 2 define a non-extreme regime where price paths are less sensitive to positioning-driven exhaustion (1).
  • Russell E-mini - Individual Market Analysis (Latest: 2026-04-07) (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the 4-week "Long Build" flow of 3.4, which reverses a seven-week streak of capital liquidation (1).
  • Price is rising (2560.10) alongside positive positioning flow, suggesting the rally is gaining sponsorship (1).
  • Stabilisation is evident as the Positioning Tension Score dropped from 3.79 in January to 1.07 currently (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A reversal to "Long Reduction" flow (negative Flow_4w) would negate the current mean-reversion thesis (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Mean reversion toward neutral positioning supported by the first positive flow impulse in two months (1).
  • Upside risk: Short covering squeeze if Speculator Z-score moves toward extreme negative levels (<-2.0).
  • Downside risk: Renewed recession hedging triggered by a spike in credit spreads or bank lending tightening (1).

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks): The methodology prioritizes 4-week flow momentum for capturing short-horizon tactical shifts (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; current positioning is neutral-to-short, providing room for expansion without immediate crowding constraints (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs domestic cyclicality and credit-sensitive equity demand (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Improving capital flow into small-caps implies a reduction in perceived funding stress or recessionary tail risk (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with credit spreads and bank lending standards to confirm broader risk-on/risk-off regimes (1).

Regime Context:

  • This is a newly entered Balanced_Long_Bias regime following an eight-week persistent short bias (1).
  • Direction of change: Strengthening as capital flow pivots from liquidation to accumulation (1).

Model Limitations:

  • Sensitivity to tactical short-horizon flows and reporting lags inherent in CoT data (1).

Data & References:

  • CME Russell 2000 E-mini (Latest: 2026-04-07) (1).
  • Influential factors: Flow_4w (3.4) and Spec_zscore (0.628) (1).
  • Public credit spread indices and bank lending surveys would improve signal depth.
